meaning: n.鲱鱼鱼骨,交叉缝式,人字形adj.箭尾形的v.(使)成箭尾形
examples: In 1900, Andre Citroen invented the herringbone gear.
The floor is a herringbone of Asian Walnut timber.
Other motifs include herringbone patterns and other geometric shapes of smooth and incised areas.
The kennels will be arranged in a herringbone pattern so the dogs cannot see each other.
Yin yang seating packs in travelers more densely than herringbone and staggered designs, British Airways contends.
